animated-pictogram-video.props: {"type":"hooks","headline":"Lifecycle Hooks","subhead":"Project-defined automation on every command boundary"}

cue.label: Hook
cue.voice: Lifecycle hooks let Kanbus pull project-defined automation before and after issue operations.

subtitle.props: {"text":"Before and after integrations with policy alignment","x":960,"y":200}

text-block.props: {"text":"Block mutating operations when a required external check fails or times out.","x":530,"y":470,"size":28,"align":"center","maxWidth":700,"font":"serif","background":"var(--card)","padding":26,"borderRadius":14,"border":"1px solid var(--border)"}

{"text":"Run notifications, analytics, and enrichment after successful commands without rolling them back.","x":530,"y":800,"size":28,"align":"center","maxWidth":700,"font":"serif","background":"var(--card)","padding":26,"borderRadius":14,"border":"1px solid var(--border)"}

cue.label: Before hooks
cue.voice: Before hooks run on mutating operations and are fail closed by default, so required checks can stop invalid transitions.

cue.label: After hooks
cue.voice: After hooks run as observers. They surface warnings if needed, while keeping successful commands successful.

cue.label: Policy alignment
cue.voice: Policy guidance is aligned as a built-in hook provider, so project hooks and policy hooks share one lifecycle engine.
